text:
ecessary correction should be applied based
on length of day and night. Thus span of Ya ma is also not
constant. As the length of day and night varies the length of
Y ama will also vary accordingly.

Sage Parasara ascribes lordship of planets to these Y ama
divisions in the following way.

- R Qi'lmtaa
[After dividing day into eight equal parts, starting from lord
of the day, yama lordship should be ascribed to planets. 8th
yama does not have any lord. The yama ascribed to Saturn is
called span ofGulika (Gulika kalavela). Similarly, night also
should be divided into eight and lordship should be ascribed
to yama divisions. In the night the lordship will start from the
5th weekday lord, counted from the day]
This is the order accepted by Madhaveeya as well, vide
sloka quoted below-
